free_text: Infinite dilution resonance integrals for some short
Near heart of core with highest flux and with
the lowest thermal to epithermal ratio.
Samples were enclosed in nylon containers, transfered
to and from the irradiating position by pneumatic
Cadmium ratio method is used to measure the
Resonance integrals were derived from measured ratios
to the thermal cross section using values for the
thermal cross sections from the chart of nuclides,
resonance integral to thermal cross section was taken
as 15.7, corresponding to a resonance integral
of 1551 b. The gold monitor was in the form of Au-Al
Counting dead time corrections were kept negligible
by restricting the maximum count-rate through varying
Shielding effects and flux gradients were avoided by
Total errors are given in the tables,
however no contribution is included for uncertainty in
the assumed value for the thermal cross sections.
To exclude errors from inaccurate half-lives, samples
with and without Cd filters were irradiated, cooled a
and measured for the same time periods.
